_comment: Global config for the modular pixel-character factory loop. A 'skeleton' is a generation-parameter profile we want to A/B before committing to a winner. For each skeleton the loop makes `characters_per_skeleton` base characters, gives each the full animation set, then generates `gear_per_slot` items for every equippable gear slot, then advances to the next skeleton.

equip._comment: Gear is rendered worn onto the character's animation frames (transfer-outfit-v2) so it's part of the motion. This is expensive (async, max 3 frames/call), so during skeleton exploration we only equip a representative set of animations on the reference character (char_00). Set animations to 'all' for a finished/winning skeleton.

budget._comment: Loop stops cleanly when the PixelLab balance drops below this, so it never hard-fails mid-job.

animations.action: standing still, idle breathing
walking
running fast
jumping straight up, arms raised
crouching down low, idle
falling downward through the air
kicking forward with one leg while standing
throwing a forward punch while standing
low sweeping kick while crouching
punching forward while crouching low
flying kick in mid air
punching forward while airborne
punching forward while running
kicking forward while running
landing softly from a short hop, slight knee bend
landing from a fall, knees bending to absorb impact
hard landing from a high fall, crouching low on impact
head snapping back, recoiling backward from a hit to the head
doubling over backward from a punch to the stomach
stumbling backward from a hit to the legs
lurching forward from a blow to the back of the head
pitching forward from a hit to the back
stumbling forward from a hit to the back of the legs
flinching while crouching, struck from the front
flinching forward while crouching, struck from behind

gear_slots._comment: Torso/arms/legs — produced by character creation, not generated separately.

style_base: cozy farming-sim RPG pixel art, Grave Seasons / Stardew Valley inspired, warm earthy palette, clean readable shapes

skeleton_variations.note: Baseline: side view, 64x64, single east direction (mirror for west), mannequin.
Taller canvas for more detail at the same view.
Top-down RPG, 4 directions — classic farming-sim movement.
Softer outline + selective shading for a gentler look.

procedural_variation._comment: When the explicit skeleton_variations list is exhausted, derive further skeletons by cycling these axes.
